summary: Set 071 Session 3 of 3 (FINAL) -- synthesis + docs + STAGED release + lesson + dogfood + close. Ran on claude-opus-4-8/medium (matches S2's routed rec for S3). NO new production code (S1 shipped the reviewer-template materiality layer; S2 the severity-anchored blocking classifier + workflow Step-6 discipline, both already verified+committed). OPERATOR DIRECTIVE (honored): do NOT publish a PyPI/Marketplace release this session (a concurrent process in another VS Code instance must not be disrupted). The release is STAGED, NOT PUBLISHED: version bumped + CHANGELOG written + committed, but NO v0.25.0 tag pushed and NO publish triggered. Confirmed both release workflows (release.yml/PyPI, publish-vscode.yml/Marketplace) are tag-triggered only, so the master-branch commits cannot fire a release. DELIVERED: (1) docs/verification-surface-strategy.md -- new section 7 (the materiality gate + Minor-non-blocking loop discipline = the calibration layer the steelman-push framing needed) + section-3 cross-ref; precise on the push-trusts-VERIFIED-token vs pull-structured-severity anti-laundering split. (2) ai_router/docs/pull-verifier.md -- 'What Set 071 added' section. (3) docs/planning/lessons-learned.md -- lesson L-071-1 (portable; cites L-069-2/L-065-1/L-070-1) + guidance-overhead header refresh. (4) ai_router/CHANGELOG.md -- [0.25.0] entry for Set 071 + a BACKFILLED [0.24.0] entry (Set 070 bumped the version + tagged v0.24.0 but never wrote its package-changelog section -- reconstructed here, clearly marked). (5) ai_router 0.24.0 -> 0.25.0 (__init__.py + pyproject.toml). (6) set-level change-log.md. (7) routed next-SESSION-SET rec (route analysis, gemini-pro -> s3-next-session-set.md): rec set-072-consolidate-apparatus (CANONICAL repo) = harden the L-069-1 deferred sibling-reader residual (path_aware_critique.py/dedicated_verification.py) + instrument the materiality-gate effect on re-verify-round count/false-positive rate; alts set-072-harden-readers / set-072-field-pilot-enablement. SUITE: full ai_router GREEN 2165 passed / 5 skipped (unchanged from S2; the 5 skips are the Set 069 S4 real-Podman-on-Windows by-design skips). SESSION VERIFICATION (cross-provider, routed_gate REQUIRED; up-front conventions block per L-064-10): 7 rounds. R1-R3 gpt-5-4 META-CONFUSION -- it treated the work-product bundle as if it should itself be a verification verdict and faulted it for 'no verdict'; root cause was the evidence framing (the full set-spec as original_task + reviewer-voice conventions primed it to expect a verdict). Reframed original_task as a crisp documentation-accuracy task + the response as a clean work-product summary, and switched the verifier to gemini-pro for fresh eyes. R4 REAL Major (ADOPTED): the docs overclaimed the anti-laundering guarantee -- 'is_blocking_verdict blocks a Major regardless of token' stated without the push-parser-trusts-VERIFIED-token caveat (the push parser returns ('VERIFIED', []) and never re-mines clean prose, operator-adjudicated in S2) -> fixed all 6 echoes (L-065-1): strategy 7.1.2/7.2, CHANGELOG [0.25.0], change-log.md intro/S2-bullet, L-071-1, + pull-verifier.md. R5 REAL Major (ADOPTED): loop-scope stated inconsistently across docs (the classifier is surface-agnostic push+pull; the Step-6 loop discipline is wired into the routed-api + Lightweight Mode-B loops) -> made strategy 7.1.3 + L-071-1 distinguish the two precisely. R6 FALSE POSITIVE (adjudicated, then clarified): claimed the template_text seam is mislocated -> VERIFIED in code the seam IS in pull_critique.py (prompt_body_of/build_instruction) and classify_framing_strength in dual_surface_verify.py CALLS it (line 433) -> the claim was correct; added a disambiguating clause anyway. R7 FALSE POSITIVE (adjudicated): claimed the new symbols are not exported from __init__.py because the S3 diff shows only the version bump -> VERIFIED all 6 symbols (is_blocking_verdict/classify_blocking/reconcile_issue_ledger/parse_nits/BlockingClassification/LedgerReconciliation) import cleanly from ai_router; they were exported in S2 commit 2132bfe (L-064-9: diff-based evidence omits already-committed context) -> + 1 non-blocking NIT (classify_framing_strength short-name vs fully-qualified in 2 places; accepted as immaterial per this set's own materiality bar). Adjudicated session verdict: VERIFIED (both real Majors R4/R5 fixed; R6/R7 code-verified false positives adjudicated against ground truth, evidence-wins per the template). The verification loop ITSELF dogfooded the set's discipline: R4/R5 were materially-justified rounds, R6/R7 were correctly adjudicated false positives that did not churn further, and a non-blocking nit was recorded not litigated. DOGFOOD (pathAwareCritique: required): pull_critique producer over HEAD~1..HEAD, providers google+openai (>=2, gate-valid path-aware-critique.json written, ok=True). google VERIFIED (0 findings); openai ISSUES_FOUND (2 Major) -- BOTH adjudicated as the L-070-1 iterative-dogfood-staleness/write-ahead pattern, NOT real defects: (F1 contract-drift) the change-log describes S3 as CLOSED/VERIFIED before close_session runs (write-ahead, true within the close sequence; the same convention every set's final change-log uses), and (F2 completeness) the critic's list_dir saw no path-aware-critique.json because the critic IS the process that writes it (the artifact is written only after both critics finish; now present). Per L-070-1, kept the final round as the gate artifact and adjudicated every finding rather than chase a clean re-run. All 7 session-verification raw outputs saved as s3-verification[-round-N].md (never edited); harnesses run_next_set.py + run_s3_verification.py. SET 071 COMPLETE (release staged for operator to publish later).

reason.specifics: This is the FINAL session of Set 071; the set closes here, so there is no next session of THIS set. The next-SESSION-SET recommendation was produced via routed analysis (s3-next-session-set.md, gemini-pro): set-072-consolidate-apparatus in the CANONICAL repo -- harden the L-069-1 deferred sibling-reader residual (the non-list `entries` iteration in path_aware_critique.py / dedicated_verification.py, a latent close-out-crash class) AND instrument the materiality gate's quantitative effect (re-verify-round count + false-positive rate) using the Set 069 benchmark as the testbed; defensible alternatives are set-072-harden-readers (minimal stability fix) and set-072-field-pilot-enablement (consumer-repo pilot enablement, where powered telemetry actually comes from). The orchestrator named here mirrors S1-S3 (opus-4-8/medium) only as a default carry-forward; the operator chooses the next set and its orchestrator, and a non-anthropic engine is defensible for fresh eyes. NOTE FOR THE OPERATOR: ai_router 0.25.0 is STAGED, not published -- when the concurrent VS Code process is clear, push tag v0.25.0 on a green-Test SHA to release to PyPI (per the publish runbook; verify the tag commit == the fixed SHA, Set 068 lesson).
